site.tagline: Honest, unhurried writing for ordinary days.

ui.noPosts: Nothing here yet. Something's on the way.
ui.footer: Made by people figuring it out right alongside you.
ui.footerLegal: General information shared in good faith — not professional, medical, legal or financial advice. © 2025 Margin Notes.

review.scoresTitle: How our testers rated each option

recipes.name: Recipes
recipes.description: Forgiving food for ordinary days and the people you feed.
pitch: Cooked in a real, busy kitchen before it reaches you
Short ingredient lists you'll actually have in
We tell you if it's worth the washing up
recipes.pitchMore: No sponsored ingredients and no fifty-step methods — just food that works on a Tuesday night.

living.name: Home & Living
living.description: Small ways to make the place you live feel a little kinder.
living.pitchTitle: What makes our home tips different?
pitch: Tried in small, rented and shared homes too
Cheap fixes first, before we ever say buy
Honest about what simply isn't worth your time
living.pitchMore: We only suggest things we've actually lived with, not products someone paid us to feature.

wellbeing.name: Wellbeing
wellbeing.description: Gentle, practical notes on rest, mood, and minding yourself.
wellbeing.pitchTitle: What makes our wellbeing notes different?
pitch: Written by people who've had the hard days too
Small steps, never a thirty-day overhaul
wellbeing.pitchMore: Realistic and kind — and never a substitute for proper support when you need it.

money.name: Money
money.description: Plain-English thoughts on spending, saving, and worrying less.
money.pitchTitle: What makes our money notes different?
pitch: Plain English, no jargon and no sales pitch
Written for real, uneven, real-life budgets
We say plainly when something isn't worth it
money.pitchMore: General guidance only — never commission-driven, and never a replacement for regulated advice.
